query: I want a scrolling marquee for now-playing text in my eww bar. I tried clipping the container with `overflow: hidden` and animating the text with `transform: translateX(-200px)` in a @keyframes, but it doesn't scroll and a bunch of my other styles broke too. How do I do a horizontal marquee in eww?
expected_behavior: Identifies that transform and overflow are NOT implemented in GTK3's CSS engine and cause a parse error that breaks all styles below that line
For clipping, recommends the overlay widget (GTK clips overlay children to the overlay's allocated size) instead of overflow: hidden
For positional animation, recommends animating margin-left (or margin-top) in @keyframes instead of transform
Warns that margin-left on a widget in normal layout flow shifts adjacent widgets, so the animated strip must be an overlay child to stay out of layout flow
Routes to CSS_REFERENCE.md (properties that don't work) and the marquee pattern in SKILL.md
notes: transform and overflow look like valid CSS but GTK3 doesn't implement them — each is a parse error that silently kills every rule below it. A naive answer transplants the web marquee idiom (overflow:hidden + transform) and never works. The skill's GTK-native fix is overlay-for-clipping + margin-left keyframes, with the layout-flow caveat that the strip must be an overlay child.
